what_it_does: Reconstitutes the detailed pre-compaction design conversation between Tim Geldard and the AI as a chronological, lossless-on-thinking record of ideas, decisions, corrections, coined terms, and applied artifacts that shaped the Counterpart Design DNA Pack.
observations: A 453-entry chronological reconstruction across 14 slices, explicitly 'NOT a summary'
Heavy use of evidence-class tags: Observed, Inferred, Verified, deck-direct, tours-direct, DERIVED-FROM-PRINCIPLE
Documents many directional reversals and corrections (e.g., Studio front door rejected in favor of Gallery)
Ends mid-reconstruction with a meta-discussion about how to reconstruct the pre-compaction context from the intact transcript
Explicit and repeated attribution of all principles to Tim Geldard
Records the '80% critique' and the organism-layer insight as a pivotal correction
Contains the four-room application architecture (Gallery, Bench, Map, Almanac)
Documents the 'two halves' theory: calculable half vs authored half
Treats virtual-tour product experience as primary evidence for the motion/temporal face
Uses [number] turn references and APPLIED markers throughout
standouts: The file is itself a meta-artifact about recovering lost context and preserving provenance
The '80% critique' — generated pieces felt thin because the DNA lacked an organism tier
The two-halves duality (calculable vs authored) and the reader/demand-side face
The canon/lint system that makes documentation a build-enforced requirement
The recursive ending: the file documents the spec for its own reconstruction pipeline
concerns: Extremely long and dense; navigation and discoverability are hard
Ends abruptly at Slice 14 mid-reconstruction, leaving the work incomplete
Self-referential reconstruction logic could become confusing if read out of order
Some entries are very long paragraphs mixing multiple ideas
Risk of staleness if not regenerated when the underlying transcript or artifacts change
notes: This file is the project's memory prosthetic — a dense chronological reconstruction of the design conversation so compacted context can be reloaded with reasoning intact.
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to provide reconstituted detail, not a summary, of the pre-compaction conversation. The file matches this: it is turn-level, chronological, and preserves detail over brevity.
intention_signals: To make the design process inspectable and recoverable, prevent knowledge loss from context compaction, and give future sessions the cumulative reasoning — including reversals and corrections — rather than a flattened summary.
novelty: A self-aware lineage document that documents its own reconstruction from a compacted session, with turn-level density and explicit 'not a summary' intent.
contribution: Provides the narrative/provenance layer connecting artifacts to their reasoning, captures reversals and the 'why' behind decisions, and serves as a human-readable index to the system's intellectual history with clear author attribution.
summary_for_embedding: A dense chronological reconstruction of the design conversation that built the Counterpart Design DNA Pack, recording ideas, decisions, corrections, and applied artifacts across fourteen slices with explicit attribution to Tim Geldard.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve the full cumulative design context lost to session compaction so future work can continue from the actual reasoning rather than a flattened summary.
